source_jobs.title: (Senior) Backend Engineer (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Our mission at Avelios is to unlock clinical data to power seamless healthcare operations for better patient care. To do so, we have built a modular software platform that digitizes and optimizes workflows in hospitals with cutting-edge technology in a user-friendly way. With our software, we enable hospitals, doctors & nurses to provide their patients with the best possible care.
We are growing fast and want to keep expanding our team and business to fundamentally digitize healthcare for the better. We appreciate different backgrounds and see diversity as one of our strengths.




THE TEAM AND ROLE

As a (Senior) Backend Engineer (all genders), you’ll help build the robust, secure infrastructure that powers our platform in clinical environments. You’ll be part of our core development team, designing scalable services, optimizing performance, and applying best practices in backend engineering to help deliver real impact in healthcare.




YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Collaborate closely with our core development team to build secure and reliable backend services

 - Design, test, deploy, and maintain critical Avelios services and infrastructure

 - Develop scalable, high-performance software in a healthcare context

 - Apply and advocate best practices in fast, secure software engineering

 - Work with modern technology to solve complex challenges in clinical digitization

 - Contribute ideas to improve system architecture, usability, and performance
   
   


YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

 - Degree in Computer Science, Informatics, Business Informatics, or equivalent practical experience

 - Proficiency in at least one of the following: Java, C++, or Rust

 - Demonstrated coding experience — GitHub contributions or personal projects are a plus

 - Eagerness to learn and work with new technologies and tools

 - Strong problem-solving skills and a hands-on, solution-oriented mindset

 - Fluency in English




YOUR BENEFITS

 - Variety in your tasks and the opportunity to take ownership

 - An international, informal team structure in a motivating start-up atmosphere, allowing you to participate directly in our customer growth story and the overall success of Avelios

 - Ability to gain valuable hands-on experience in a startup backed by leading international VCs

 - Support for your personal development through continuous learning and feedback

 - Set your own rhythm with flexible working hours and trust-based time tracking

 - A virtual stock option package (VSOP) that lets you participate in Avelios’ long-term value creation

 - A monthly meal allowance up to 75 euros, full coverage of the Deutschlandticket, Urban Sports Club or a subsidized Wellpass membership, a bike leasing program and Corporate Benefits that give you access to discounts from a wide range of providers in various areas

source_jobs.title: Working Student Software Engineering Frontend (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Our mission at Avelios is to unlock clinical data to power seamless healthcare operations for better patient care. To do so, we have built a modular software platform that digitizes and optimizes workflows in hospitals with cutting-edge technology in a user-friendly way. With our software, we enable hospitals, doctors & nurses to provide their patients with the best possible care.
We are growing fast and want to keep expanding our team and business to fundamentally digitize healthcare for the better. We appreciate different backgrounds and see diversity as one of our strengths.




THE TEAM AND ROLE

As a Working Student in Frontend Engineering (all genders), you will join our development team and gain hands-on experience building complex, large-scale software in a mission-critical environment. Working alongside experienced engineers, you’ll help shape the digital infrastructure that supports real-world clinical care — learning how to deliver secure, scalable, and high-quality code with real impact.




YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Collaborate directly with our core development team on real product features

 - Design, test, deploy, and maintain backend or frontend components of Avelios services

 - Learn how to build and maintain large-scale clinical software infrastructure

 - Work with modern technologies in a high-impact healthcare setting

 - Learn and apply best practices in secure and efficient software engineering

 - Contribute ideas to improve performance, usability, and user experience




YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

 - Currently enrolled in Informatics, Computer Science, Business Informatics, or a related field

 - Experience in at least one of the following: Swift, TypeScript or React

 - Demonstrated coding experience (e.g. GitHub contributions) is a plus

 - Passion for learning and exploring new technologies

 - Solution-oriented mindset with a strong focus on execution

 - Fluency in German and/or English




YOUR BENEFITS

 - Variety in your tasks and the opportunity to take ownership

 - Flexible working hours

 - Support for your personal development through continuous learning and feedback and high chances to transition into a full-time role if the collaboration is successful

 - An international, informal team structure in a motivating start-up atmosphere, allowing you to participate directly in our customer growth story and the overall success of Avelios

 - Ability to gain valuable hands-on experience in a startup backed by leading international VCs

 - A monthly meal allowance up to 75 euros, and Corporate Benefits that give you access to discounts from a wide range of providers in various areas

source_jobs.title: DevOps Engineer (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Our mission at Avelios is to unlock clinical data to power seamless healthcare operations for better patient care. To do so, we have built a modular software platform that digitizes and optimizes workflows in hospitals with cutting-edge technology in a user-friendly way. With our software, we enable hospitals, doctors & nurses to provide their patients with the best possible care.
We are growing fast and want to keep expanding our team and business to fundamentally digitize healthcare for the better. We appreciate different backgrounds and see diversity as one of our strengths.




THE TEAM AND ROLE

We’re looking for a (Senior) DevOps Engineer (all genders) to join our infrastructure team and take ownership of deploying and operating our software in both cloud and clinical environments. You’ll collaborate closely with developers and product teams to ensure our systems are scalable, secure, and reliable. This role is key to transforming healthcare delivery with seamless and stable technology.




YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Collaborate closely with our core development team to roll out new features and services

 - Design, test, deploy, and maintain the infrastructure that powers Avelios in hospitals and the cloud

 - Operate and improve large-scale deployments with high availability and performance requirements

 - Apply best practices in observability, incident response, and fault tolerance

 - Ensure our systems are secure, reliable, and scalable in clinical environments

 - Continuously propose and implement improvements to performance, automation, and tooling
   
   


YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

 - Degree in Informatics, Computer Science, or a related field — or equivalent hands-on experience

 - Solid experience with Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD pipelines, and Linux environments

 - Familiarity with monitoring, alerting, and logging best practices

 - Experience with modern toolchains and infrastructure-as-code (e.g., Terraform, Ansible) is a plus

 - Strong interest in learning new technologies and working in a fast-paced, high-impact setting

 - Solution-oriented mindset and a pragmatic, hands-on working style

 - Fluency in English; German is a plus but not required
   
   


YOUR BENEFITS

 - Variety in your tasks and the opportunity to take ownership

 - An international, informal team structure in a motivating start-up atmosphere, allowing you to participate directly in our customer growth story and the overall success of Avelios

 - Ability to gain valuable hands-on experience in a startup backed by leading international VCs

 - Support for your personal development through continuous learning and feedback

 - Set your own rhythm with flexible working hours and trust-based time tracking

 - A virtual stock option package (VSOP) that lets you participate in Avelios’ long-term value creation

 - A monthly meal allowance up to 75 euros, full coverage of the Deutschlandticket, Urban Sports Club or a subsidized Wellpass membership, a bike leasing program and Corporate Benefits that give you access to discounts from a wide range of providers in various areas

source_jobs.title: (Senior) Sales Manager (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Our mission at Avelios is to unlock clinical data to power seamless healthcare operations for better patient care. To do so, we have built a modular software platform that digitizes and optimizes workflows in hospitals with cutting-edge technology in a user-friendly way. With our software, we enable hospitals, doctors & nurses to provide their patients with the best possible care.
We are growing fast and want to keep expanding our team and business to fundamentally digitize healthcare for the better. We appreciate different backgrounds and see diversity as one of our strengths.




THE TEAM AND YOUR ROLE

To further shape the future of healthcare, we are looking for a (Senior) Sales Manager (all genders) who will actively drive our sales pipeline and help expand Avelios’ impact across the clinical sector. In this role, you play a key part in growing our customer base and ensuring that hospitals across Germany benefit from our software platform.

You will work closely with Marketing, Business Development, Product, and Customer Success to manage the entire sales cycle — from identifying and qualifying prospects to closing deals and supporting long-term customer relationships. This role offers a unique opportunity to help shape the commercial strategy of a fast-growing health-tech company and make a meaningful contribution to improving healthcare delivery.




YOUR TASKS

 - Taking full ownership of the entire B2B sales process — from first contact to contract signature

 - Working closely with internal teams to ensure a seamless sales experience and successful implementation

 - Qualifying leads, advising potential customers, and converting them into long-term, satisfied partnerships

 - Developing tailored proposals that align customer needs with Avelios’ business objectives

 - Creating and refining sales strategies, presentations, and market analyses to expand the pipeline

 - Building and maintaining a strong network within the healthcare and hospital ecosystem

 - Tracking and reporting key sales KPIs to continuously improve forecasting and sales processes

 - Acting as a trusted point of contact for prospects and customers, clearly communicating product value to diverse stakeholders




YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

 - Completed degree in business, engineering, medicine, or a comparable field

 - Strong experience in B2B software sales — ideally in healthcare or the hospital environment

 - Proven track record of acquiring new customers and driving measurable commercial impact

 - Excellent presentation and negotiation skills across different audiences

 - Strong interest in digital health and solid technical understanding of software products

 - Structured, solution-oriented mindset with strong execution capability

 - Independent, hands-on working style and enthusiasm for driving customer success

 - Fluent German skills and very good English skills




YOUR BENEFITS

 - Variety in your tasks and the opportunity to take ownership

 - An international, informal team structure in a motivating start-up atmosphere, allowing you to participate directly in our customer growth story and the overall success of Avelios

 - Ability to gain valuable hands-on experience in a startup backed by leading international VCs

 - Support for your personal development through continuous learning and feedback

 - Set your own rhythm with flexible working hours and trust-based time tracking

 - A virtual stock option package (VSOP) that lets you participate in Avelios’ long-term value creation

 - A monthly meal allowance up to 75 euros, full coverage of the Deutschlandticket, Urban Sports Club or a subsidized Wellpass membership, a bike leasing program and Corporate Benefits that give you access to discounts from a wide range of providers in various areas

source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Our mission at Avelios is to unlock clinical data to power seamless healthcare operations for better patient care. To do so, we have built a modular software platform that digitizes and optimizes workflows in hospitals with cutting-edge technology in a user-friendly way. With our software, we enable hospitals, doctors & nurses to provide their patients with the best possible care.
We are growing fast and want to keep expanding our team and business to fundamentally digitize healthcare for the better. We appreciate different backgrounds and see diversity as one of our strengths.




THE TEAM AND YOUR ROLE

As an IT System Administrator (all genders), you play a central role in the ongoing development and security of our internal IT infrastructure. You work at the intersection of IT security, user support, and system administration, ensuring that our teams can operate in a stable, secure, and modern IT environment. You contribute your technical expertise to security concepts, onboarding processes, and our overall system landscape.




YOUR TASKS

 - Enhancing security for Linux, Windows, Mac, and mobile devices

 - Supporting users with security-related questions and IT onboarding

 - Acting as the first point of contact for colleagues with hardware, software, or network issues

 - Developing and managing backup and recovery solutions

 - Monitoring and optimizing system performance, including capacity planning

 - Creating technical documentation for the entire IT environment

 - Actively contributing to the development and implementation of security concepts as part of various certifications




YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

 - Completed vocational training as an IT specialist or a completed degree in an IT-related field (or similar)

 - Basic knowledge of network infrastructure and network services

 - Initial experience with Linux, Apple, and Windows systems is an advantage

 - Strong analytical thinking and a structured, precise way of working

 - High level of innovation, self-initiative, and willingness to learn new technologies

 - Good communication skills and strong team spirit

 - Fluent in German and English
   
   


YOUR BENEFITS

 - Variety in your tasks and the opportunity to take ownership

 - An international, informal team structure in a motivating start-up atmosphere, allowing you to participate directly in our customer growth story and the overall success of Avelios

source_jobs.title: Machine Learning Engineer (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Our mission at Avelios is to unlock clinical data to power seamless healthcare operations for better patient care. To do so, we have built a modular software platform that digitizes and optimizes workflows in hospitals with cutting-edge technology in a user-friendly way. With our software, we enable hospitals, doctors & nurses to provide their patients with the best possible care.
We are growing fast and want to keep expanding our team and business to fundamentally digitize healthcare for the better. We appreciate different backgrounds and see diversity as one of our strengths.




THE TEAM AND ROLE

As a Machine Learning Engineer (all genders), you will drive the integration of intelligent systems into real-world clinical environments. Collaborating with our interdisciplinary development teams, your role will focus on bringing state-of-the-art models (LLMs, ASR, and more), into production and embedding them directly into Avelios software to improve everyday medical workflows. This is a unique opportunity to shape how AI transforms the way clinicians work.




YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Collaborate with our core development team to bring machine learning into production

 - Design and train ML models on large-scale clinical datasets

 - Integrate AI-powered solutions (LLMs, ASR, RAG, Embedding, and more in the future) into real medical workflows

 - Evaluate, benchmark, and fine-tune existing models to fit clinical use cases

 - Work with Federated Machine Learning frameworks for privacy-preserving model training

 - Collaborate with leading academic and research institutions

 - Own the reliability, performance, and quality of AI features in production

 - Leverage access to high-performance computing infrastructure to build robust ML pipelines




YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

 - Degree in Informatics, Computer Science, or a related field - or equivalent practical experience

 - Strong coding background - GitHub contributions are a plus

 - Hands-on experience deploying ML systems to production using PyTorch, LangChain, and Pydantic, including LLMs, ASR models, and RAG pipelines with vector databases (e.g., Qdrant).

 - Publications at top conferences or demonstrated cutting-edge results in applied ML (e.g., Speech Recognition or NLP) is a plus

 - Interest in new technologies and their application to real-world challenges

 - Fluency in English, German is a plus
   
   


YOUR BENEFITS

 - Variety in your tasks and the opportunity to take ownership

 - An international, informal team structure in a motivating start-up atmosphere, allowing you to participate directly in our customer growth story and the overall success of Avelios

 - Ability to gain valuable hands-on experience in a startup backed by leading international VCs

 - Support for your personal development through continuous learning and feedback

 - Set your own rhythm with flexible working hours and trust-based time tracking

 - A virtual stock option package (VSOP) that lets you participate in Avelios’ long-term value creation

 - A monthly meal allowance up to 75 euros, full coverage of the Deutschlandticket, Urban Sports Club or a subsidized Wellpass membership, a bike leasing program and Corporate Benefits that give you access to discounts from a wide range of providers in various areas

source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Our mission at Avelios is to unlock clinical data to power seamless healthcare operations for better patient care. To do so, we have built a modular software platform that digitizes and optimizes workflows in hospitals with cutting-edge technology in a user-friendly way. With our software, we enable hospitals, doctors & nurses to provide their patients with the best possible care.
We are growing fast and want to keep expanding our team and business to fundamentally digitize healthcare for the better. We appreciate different backgrounds and see diversity as one of our strengths.




THE TEAM AND YOUR ROLE

As a Nursing Professional for Software Testing (QA) (all genders), you ensure that our software not only works reliably but also delivers an excellent user experience. Your testing helps us identify issues early, optimize usability, and continuously improve the stability of our platform. In close collaboration with our product and engineering teams, you directly influence the quality of our software and thereby contribute to a better digital care experience in hospitals.




YOUR TASKS

 - Testing our software for functionality, design, usability, and content accuracy

 - Executing structured tests based on defined requirements and test plans

 - Identifying and precisely documenting all types of issues — from spelling mistakes to technical bugs

 - Creating Jira tickets for identified deviations and tracking the progress of issue resolution

 - Developing and maintaining test plans, test cases, and test reports

 - Conducting regression tests for resolved issues
   
   


YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience in software testing, test methods, and quality assurance

 - Initial experience in nursing is desirable

 - Technical understanding, perseverance, and a strong commitment to quality

 - Highly structured and self-driven working style

 - Strong attention to detail and passion for precise results

 - Solution-oriented mindset and hands-on mentality

 - Experience with Jira is a plus

 - Fluent in German and English




YOUR BENEFITS

 - Variety in your tasks and the opportunity to take ownership

 - An international, informal team structure in a motivating start-up atmosphere, allowing you to participate directly in our customer growth story and the overall success of Avelios

source_jobs.title: Billing Specialist/ Patientenabrechnung (all genders) in healthcare
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Our mission at Avelios is to unlock clinical data to power seamless healthcare operations for better patient care. To do so, we have built a modular software platform that digitizes and optimizes workflows in hospitals with cutting-edge technology in a user-friendly way. With our software, we enable hospitals, doctors & nurses to provide their patients with the best possible care.
We are growing fast and want to keep expanding our team and business to fundamentally digitize healthcare for the better. We appreciate different backgrounds and see diversity as one of our strengths.




THE TEAM AND YOUR ROLE

As a billing specialist/ Experte Patientenabrechnung (all genders) in healthcare, you are a key expert at Avelios and contribute your knowledge from hospital billing directly to our product and customer processes.

With your experience in billing, coding, or process management in hospitals, you understand the workflows and challenges of everyday clinical work. You use this knowledge to further develop our software solutions together with our Medical Team and the product managers – especially the billing module, which simplifies processes and significantly eases daily work in hospitals.

At the same time, you are a reliable point of contact for our customers and project teams when it comes to billing topics, process questions, or the implementation of regulatory requirements. You support the introduction of our solutions, accompany projects from a professional perspective, and help ensure that our customers can optimally design their billing processes.

With your expertise, you help us bridge the gap between clinical practice and the digital product world – actively shaping how hospital billing will work in the future.




YOUR TASKS

 - Analysis of clinical billing processes and translation into clear requirements and rules for our billing module

 - Professional design, expansion, and quality assurance of content, validation logic, workflows, and texts

 - Monitoring of legal and regulatory requirements and derivation of resulting changes

 - Close collaboration with the Medical Team and product management as a professional source for decisions

 - Professional support for projects in the initiation phase: requirements analysis, development of suitable solution approaches, professional demos, and contributions to service descriptions/scoping

 - Professional support during implementation and operation for customers: process analysis, parameterization/customizing, and practical execution

 - Training and enablement for users on billing, coding, and best practices

 - KPI and error pattern analysis in operations, derivation of concrete improvements, and coordination as a professional escalation point




YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

 - Completed studies or vocational training in health economics, healthcare management, business administration, public health, or a medical or nursing qualification with experience in hospital billing

 - In-depth understanding of billing logic and processes in inpatient and outpatient service areas, along with confident handling of common billing systems in healthcare

 - Experience in translating professional requirements into clear specifications and in professional quality assurance

 - Strong communication and teamwork skills and enthusiasm for interdisciplinary collaboration

 - Enjoyment of customer- and project-oriented work (workshops, process analysis, trainings)

 - Structured, analytical, and conceptual way of working

 - Very good German and good English skills




YOUR BENEFITS

 - Variety in your tasks and the opportunity to take ownership

 - An international, informal team structure in a motivating start-up atmosphere, allowing you to participate directly in our customer growth story and the overall success of Avelios

 - Ability to gain valuable hands-on experience in a startup backed by leading international VCs

 - Support for your personal development through continuous lear

Our mission at Avelios is to unlock clinical data to power seamless healthcare operations for better patient care. To do so, we have built a modular software platform that digitizes and optimizes workflows in hospitals with cutting-edge technology in a user-friendly way. With our software, we enable hospitals, doctors & nurses to provide their patients with the best possible care.
We are growing fast and want to keep expanding our team and business to fundamentally digitize healthcare for the better. We appreciate different backgrounds and see diversity as one of our strengths.




THE TEAM AND YOUR ROLE

We are looking for a Clinical Project Manager (all genders) to lead the implementation of our software in hospitals. In this role, you act as the interface between internal teams and external stakeholders, ensuring that projects run smoothly, on time, and with real value for clinical users. You play a central role in transforming innovative technology into successful customer projects. This role includes occasional travel to customer sites.




YOUR TASKS

 - Responsibility for managing various customer projects to ensure the successful implementation of our software in hospitals

 - Close collaboration with our Sales, Product, and Engineering teams to ensure smooth project execution and ongoing customer success

 - Management and documentation of projects throughout the entire lifecycle — from initial planning to final handover — with a strong focus on deliverables and timelines

 - Collection of requirements from all project stakeholders to deliver effective outcomes, as well as identification and mitigation of potential risks

 - Continuous improvement of internal processes and tools to ensure efficient and successful customer projects, along with active contribution to the growth of our project team

 - Visualization and presentation of key performance indicators for customers to create success stories and clearly communicate the value delivered




YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

 - Completed degree in health sciences or medicine

 - Relevant experience in project management, ideally as a project manager for complex B2B software solutions in healthcare or as a consultant in this field

 - Professional experience in hospitals is an advantage

 - Ability to manage multiple projects involving different internal and external stakeholders

 - Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to present complex topics in an understandable way

 - Solution-oriented mindset combined with an independent and structured working style

 - Fluent German and English skills




YOUR BENEFITS

 - A promising opportunity to expand our Project Team in a scale-up backed by leading international VCs, SAP and Fresenius

 - An international, informal team structure in a motivating scale-up atmosphere, allowing you to participate directly in our growth story and the overall success of Avelios

 - A virtual stock option package (VSOP) that lets you benefit from Avelios’ long-term value creation

 - A monthly meal allowance of up to 75 euros, full coverage of the Deutschlandticket, Urban Sports Club or a subsidized Wellpass membership, a bike leasing program and Corporate Benefits that give you access to discounts from a wide range of providers in various

source_jobs.title: Pre-Sales Consultant for Clinical Software Solutions (all genders)

THE TEAM AND YOUR ROLE

To continue shaping the future of healthcare, we are looking for a dedicated Pre-Sales Consultant (all genders) who plays an active role in driving our customer growth from the very beginning and contributes significantly to presenting and promoting our clinical software solutions.




YOUR TASKS

 - Analyzing the processes and needs of potential hospital customers and demonstrating the concrete value our platform delivers

 - Presenting our software solutions in a targeted, relatable way with a strong understanding of clinical realities

 - Supporting the sales team with your subject-matter expertise throughout the entire sales process, contributing directly to overall success

 - Bringing feedback from the market back into our product development and actively helping to further develop our platform

 - Representing Avelios at trade fairs, events, and on-site customer meetings




YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

 - Profound experience in clinical and administrative hospital environments, ideally gained through professional experience or project work in healthcare

 - Strong communication skills, empathy, and the ability to explain complex topics in a simple and audience-appropriate way

 - Enjoyment in engaging with customers and collaboratively shaping practical digital solutions

 - Basic technical understanding is helpful but not required — more important is the willingness to learn and dive into our platform

 - Very good German skills and good English skills

 - Willingness to travel for customer meetings and events




YOUR BENEFITS

 - Variety in your tasks and the opportunity to take ownership

source_jobs.title: Working Student Product Management (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Our mission at Avelios is to unlock clinical data to power seamless healthcare operations for better patient care. To do so, we have built a modular software platform that digitizes and optimizes workflows in hospitals with cutting-edge technology in a user-friendly way. With our software, we enable hospitals, doctors & nurses to provide their patients with the best possible care.
We are growing fast and want to keep expanding our team and business to fundamentally digitize healthcare for the better. We appreciate different backgrounds and see diversity as one of our strengths.




THE TEAM AND ROLE

As a Working Student Product Management (all genders), you will become part of a cross-functional team that brings together medical expertise, design, and engineering to build products that solve real problems in clinical practice. You’ll support our product managers in shaping the backlog, refining features, and driving the development process forward — all while gaining deep insights into agile product work in a high-impact healthcare setting.




YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Cross-functional Product Development: Join a cross-functional team of engineers, medical experts, and designers to help develop outstanding software, collaborate with the medical team to understand user needs and iterate on product ideas and prototypes, and build a deep understanding of user workflows to inform product decisions.

 - Product & Agile Collaboration: Assist in managing the product backlog, translate requirements into clear user stories, support engineers in day-to-day development, and help facilitate backlog refinement, sprint planning, and retrospective meetings to ensure smooth and high-quality releases.

 - Regulatory & Certification Support: Support the team in navigating regulatory and certification requirements and translating them into implementable product tasks and documentation.

 - Detail-Oriented Compliance Work: Enjoy working on detail-oriented topics such as specifications, compliance checks, and certification-ready evidence with a structured and patient approach.
   
   


YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

 - Currently enrolled in a business, technology, health sciences or a related field of study

 - Experience with agile methodologies and tools (or strong willingness to learn quickly)

 - Strong communication and collaboration skills; comfortable working closely with engineers and domain experts

 - Genuine interest in building digital health solutions that create real-world value

 - Proactive mindset with a hands-on, reliable working style

 - Fluency in German and English







YOUR BENEFITS

 - Variety in your tasks and the opportunity to take ownership

 - Flexible working hours

 - Support for your personal development through continuous learning and feedback and high chances to transition into a full-time role if the collaboration is successful

 - An international, informal team structure in a motivating start-up atmosphere, allowing you to participate directly in our customer growth story and the overall success of Avelios

 - Ability to gain valuable hands-on experience in a startup backed by leading international VCs

 - A monthly meal allowance up to 75 euros, and Corporate Benefits that give you access to discounts from a wide range of providers in various areas

Working closely with engineering, project and product management and sales, you develop customer-specific and technically feasible solution concepts.




YOUR TASKS

 - Creating technical concepts, documentation, and solution descriptions for proposals and tenders

 - Acting as the primary technical contact for customers during sales and project phases

YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

 - Completed degree in computer science or a comparable qualification

 - Strong understanding of APIs, interfaces, and system architecture

 - Knowledge of core IT and infrastructure concepts (e.g. cloud, on-premise, VPN, networking)

THE TEAM AND YOUR ROLE

As a (Senior) Talent Acquisition Manager (all genders), you are part of our People team and play a key role in driving Avelios’ growth. You will take ownership of our recruiting activities and act as the primary point of contact for potential new colleagues.




YOUR TASKS

You are the go-to person when it comes to finding the right talent for Avelios - from first contact to the final offer. You work closely with our People Partners and Hiring Managers. Your responsibilities include:

 - Active sourcing: Identifying and approaching the right candidates through LinkedIn and other channels - focusing on quality, not volume

 - End-to-end recruiting: Managing complete hiring processes, from alignment with hiring teams to offer negotiation and contract creation

 - Candidate experience: Ensuring a smooth, respectful, and positive process so that candidates remember Avelios for the right reasons

 - Sparring partner: Supporting our People Partners with recruiting for complex or strategically important roles




YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

 - Completed degree in Business, Social Sciences, (Business) Psychology, or a comparable field from a top university

 - Strong communication skills, empathy, and a confident, engaging presence

 - At least 2 years of experience in recruiting and active sourcing, ideally in a startup environment

 - Experience with Ashby as an ATS is a plus, but not required

 - Enjoy working in a fast-paced startup environment with a hands-on attitude

 - Very good German and English skills




YOUR BENEFITS

 - Variety in your tasks and the opportunity to take ownership

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Our mission at Avelios is to unlock clinical data to power seamless healthcare operations for better patient care. To do so, we have built a modular software platform that digitizes and optimizes workflows in hospitals with cutting-edge technology in a user-friendly way. With our software, we enable hospitals, doctors & nurses to provide their patients with the best possible care.
We are growing fast and want to keep expanding our team and business to fundamentally digitize healthcare for the better. We appreciate different backgrounds and see diversity as one of our strengths.




THE TEAM AND ROLE

As a Senior Product Manager (all genders) you will take end-to-end responsibility for the product strategy and further development of our core product areas within the Avelios platform — from discovery to delivery to rollout. You will work closely with Engineering, Design, our Medical team, customers, and partners, translating market and user needs into scalable, robust product features.

This is a hands-on, high-impact role where product thinking meets digital transformation in healthcare.




YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own and continuously evolve the roadmap for a dedicated module within Patient Treatment and Billing, balancing customer commitments, regulatory requirements, and strategic product progress

 - Turn clinical user needs and market signals into clear product decisions by defining problem statements, outcomes, epics, user stories, and measurable acceptance criteria—so teams can build robust workflows that fit everyday clinical practice

 - Proactively orchestrate customer and regulatory input across discovery, specification, and prioritisation, ensuring medical correctness and practical usability are embedded in new development

 - Drive cross-functional alignment with Engineering, Design, and Implementation to enable predictable delivery: clarify scope, dependencies, and handovers; improve planning reliability; and raise quality and efficiency for the components you own

 - Plan and execute launches and enablement for your features by coordinating release readiness (documentation, training, internal roll-out prep), maintaining crisp stakeholder communication, and supporting go-to-market activities with the commercial and delivery teams




YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

 - Degree in a business or technology-related field, e.g. Computer Science, Medical Informatics or a comparable field

 - 5+ years of product management experience in B2B SaaS or enterprise startups

 - Proven excellence in product management processes: structured discovery, data-driven prioritisation, and reliable delivery

 - Strong analytical and decisive working style with a systematic problem-solving approach

 - Demonstrated ability to manage multiple initiatives in parallel and align diverse stakeholders

 - Solid technical understanding of APIs, data flows, and system integrations is a plus

 - Passion for intuitive, modern digital products in healthcare

 - Fluency in English (spoken and written)




YOUR BENEFITS

 - Variety in your tasks and the opportunity to take ownership

 - An international, informal team structure in a motivating start-up atmosphere, allowing you to participate directly in our customer growth story and the overall success of Avelios

 - Ability to gain valuable hands-on experience in a startup backed by leading international VCs

 - Support for your personal development through continuous learning and feedback

 - Set your own rhythm with flexible working hours and trust-based time tracking

 - A virtual stock option package (VSOP) that lets you participate in Avelios’ long-term value creation

 - A monthly meal allowance up to 75 euros, full coverage of the Deutschlandticket, Urban Sports Club or a subsidized Wellpass membership, a bike leasing program and Corporate Benefits that give you access to discounts from a wide range of providers in various areas

source_jobs.title: Automated Software Tester (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Our mission at Avelios is to unlock clinical data to power seamless healthcare operations for better patient care. To do so, we have built a modular software platform that digitizes and optimizes workflows in hospitals with cutting-edge technology in a user-friendly way. With our software, we enable hospitals, doctors & nurses to provide their patients with the best possible care.
We are growing fast and want to keep expanding our team and business to fundamentally digitize healthcare for the better. We appreciate different backgrounds and see diversity as one of our strengths.




THE TEAM AND ROLE

The purpose of this role is to ensure the quality and reliability of our software solutions in system simulation, cloud services, and data management by planning, designing, and executing thorough tests. Success in this role means delivering defect-free releases, effectively driving test automation, and providing actionable insights from test results to improve overall software quality. The role also exists to support the team and project leaders with technical guidance on testing and quality assurance, ensuring smooth integration and deployment of software components. Ultimately, success is measured by high-quality software, efficient testing processes, and confident, informed project decisions.




YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design, implement, and maintain automated UI tests for software solutions in system simulation, cloud services, and data management.

 - Build and extend Page Objects for UI components, providing stable, reusable interactions for automated tests.

 - Create, organize, and execute test cases based on Page Objects to validate workflows and user interactions.

 - Analyze test results, report defects, and continuously improve test automation coverage and reliability.

 - Collaborate with team members to advise on testing best practices, support development of new Page Objects, and ensure high-quality software delivery.




YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

 - Degree in IT Security, Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or a related field

 - Some years of experience in software testing, ideally with automated UI testing in web applications; experience building and maintaining reusable test components and workflows

 - Strong experience with TypeScript and automated testing frameworks, preferably Playwright; solid understanding of Page Object design patterns and test automation best practices

 - Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, hands-on and solution-oriented mindset, ability to collaborate with team members and advise on testing approaches

 - Experience in creating, maintaining, and extending Page Objects for complex UI components; willingness to learn new tools or frameworks quickly

 - Fluency in English and German




YOUR BENEFITS

 - Variety in your tasks and the opportunity to take ownership

 - An international, informal team structure in a motivating start-up atmosphere, allowing you to participate directly in our customer growth story and the overall success of Avelios

 - Ability to gain valuable hands-on experience in a startup backed by leading international VCs

 - Support for your personal development through continuous learning and feedback

 - Set your own rhythm with flexible working hours and trust-based time tracking

 - A virtual stock option package (VSOP) that lets you participate in Avelios’ long-term value creation

 - A monthly meal allowance up to 75 euros, full coverage of the Deutschlandticket, Urban Sports Club or a subsidized Wellpass membership, a bike leasing program and Corporate Benefits that give you access to discounts from a wide range of providers in various areas

source_jobs.title: Medical Consultant (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Our mission at Avelios is to unlock clinical data to power seamless healthcare operations for better patient care. To do so, we have built a modular software platform that digitizes and optimizes workflows in hospitals with cutting-edge technology in a user-friendly way. With our software, we enable hospitals, doctors & nurses to provide their patients with the best possible care.
We are growing fast and want to keep expanding our team and business to fundamentally digitize healthcare for the better. We appreciate different backgrounds and see diversity as one of our strengths.




THE TEAM AND ROLE

As a Medical Consultant (all genders), you will take responsibility for shaping the clinical and process-related foundation of customer projects after contract closure. Acting as a key clinical expert within the Project team, you work closely with hospital stakeholders and internal teams during the project implementation phase to enable successful go-lives.




YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Managing key stakeholders across customers, the Project team, and the Product team throughout the configuration phase

 - Leading clinical process and requirements workshops with hospital stakeholders during the configuration phase, including software demos

 - Representing and consistently applying the Avelios best-practice standard across existing modules

 - Aligning clinical target processes with customers and defining clear go-live and rollout scopes

 - Identifying needs for product adjustments and coordinating closely with the Product team to enable scalable improvements




YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

 - Several years of professional experience in hospitals (e.g. physicians, doctors, nursing, functional departments, controlling) or extensive consulting experience in hospital transformation or IT projects

 - Strong understanding of hospital workflows and the ability to structure and analyze complex clinical and operational requirements

 - Excellent communication skills, including confident customer communication, the ability to moderate workshops, explain complex topics clearly, and handle conflicts in a professional and empathetic manner

 - Experience in hospital digitalization projects, including working with hospital information systems (KIS) and translating clinical requirements into structured artifacts (e.g. workflows, use cases, requirements), is a plus.

 - Fluency in German and English (spoken and written)




YOUR BENEFITS

 - A promising opportunity to establish a Medical Consulting Team in a scale-up backed by leading international VCs, SAP and Fresenius

 - An international, informal team structure in a motivating scale-up atmosphere, allowing you to participate directly in our growth story and the overall success of Avelios

 - A virtual stock option package (VSOP) that lets you benefit from Avelios’ long-term value creation

 - A monthly meal allowance of up to 75 euros, full coverage of the Deutschlandticket, Urban Sports Club or a subsidized Wellpass membership, a bike leasing program and Corporate Benefits that give you access to discounts from a wide range of providers in various

source_jobs.title: Executive Assistant (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Our mission at Avelios is to unlock clinical data to power seamless healthcare operations for better patient care. To do so, we have built a modular software platform that digitizes and optimizes workflows in hospitals with cutting-edge technology in a user-friendly way. With our software, we enable hospitals, doctors & nurses to provide their patients with the best possible care.
We are growing fast and want to keep expanding our team and business to fundamentally digitize healthcare for the better. We appreciate different backgrounds and see diversity as one of our strengths.




THE TEAM AND ROLE

As an Executive Assistant (all genders) to our CEO and CTO, you ensure their effectiveness by enabling focus on the company’s highest priorities. The role provides structured, reliable, and proactive support across agenda management and execution follow-up. Acting as a trusted partner, the Executive Assistant creates transparency, alignment, and operational efficiency at the executive level.




YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Support the CEO and CTO in managing day-to-day priorities, coordinating schedules, preparing materials, and ensuring smooth administrative operations

 - Prepare, structure and follow up on executive meetings and board interactions — including scheduling, agenda coordination, documentation, and tracking of action items

 - Act as a central hub for information flow to and from the CEO and CTO, gaining deep insights into company-wide initiatives and ensuring relevant information is shared in a timely manner

 - Handle confidential and sensitive information with a high degree of discretion, professionalism, and attention to detail




YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

 - Degree in Business Administration or similar studies at top university

 - Strong interest in supporting top-level executives in a dynamic environment

 - Solution-oriented mindset with a strong focus on delivering impact

 - Trusted with highly sensitive and confidential information

 - Excellent communication skills with fluency in German and English




YOUR BENEFITS

 - Promising opportunity to gain deep, behind-the-scenes executive insights in a scale-up backed by leading international VCs and SAP

 - An international, informal team structure in a motivating scale-up atmosphere, allowing you to participate directly in the overall success of Avelios

 - Set your own rhythm with flexible working hours and trust-based time tracking

 - A virtual stock option package (VSOP) that lets you participate in Avelios’ long-term value creation

 - A monthly meal allowance up to 75 euros, full coverage of the Deutschlandticket, Urban Sports Club or a subsidized Wellpass membership, a bike leasing program and Corporate Benefits that give you access to discounts from a wide range of providers in various areas

source_jobs.title: Intern Founders Associate (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Our mission at Avelios is to unlock clinical data to power seamless healthcare operations for better patient care. To do so, we have built a modular software platform that digitizes and optimizes workflows in hospitals with cutting-edge technology in a user-friendly way. With our software, we enable hospitals, doctors & nurses to provide their patients with the best possible care.
We are growing fast and want to keep expanding our team and business to fundamentally digitize healthcare for the better. We appreciate different backgrounds and see diversity as one of our strengths.




THE TEAM AND ROLE

To continue to shape the future of healthcare, we are looking for an Intern Founders Associate (all genders) to support our operations, technology strategy and hiring in collaboration with our Co-Founders.




YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Strategic project work: You will plan and implement projects in close cooperation with the founders

 - Market and competition analyses: You will be conducting analyses of target markets, competitors, and products in order to derive strategic recommendations

 - Product Strategy: Analyze and categorize potential product partners, evaluate inbound partnership requests, and conduct deep dives into specific product benefits

 - Process optimization: You will contribute to the further development and professionalization of internal processes and system landscapes to increase efficiency

 - AI Operations & automation initiatives: Identify and evaluate opportunities to integrate AI and automation into core business and operational processes
   
   


YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

 - Studying in business, physics, maths or anything connected at a top tier business school or in a similarly competitive environment

 - Experience regarding project management and/or operations, ideally in a startup, consulting firm or a similar dynamic environment

 - Structured way of working, organizational talent and very independent working style

 - Willingness to learn, adapt and execute quickly

 - Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities, with attention to detail

 - Strong skills in all common office programs

 - Excellent communication skills in German and English
   
   


YOUR BENEFITS

 - Variety in your tasks and the opportunity to take ownership

 - Flexible working hours

 - Support for your personal development through continuous learning and feedback and high chances to transition into a full-time role if the collaboration is successful

 - An international, informal team structure in a motivating start-up atmosphere, allowing you to participate directly in our customer growth story and the overall success of Avelios

 - Ability to gain valuable hands-on experience in a startup backed by leading international VCs

 - A monthly meal allowance up to 75 euros, and Corporate Benefits that give you access to discounts from a wide range of providers in various areas

source_jobs.title: Working Student Compliance Management (all genders)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Our mission at Avelios is to unlock clinical data to power seamless healthcare operations for better patient care. To do so, we have built a modular software platform that digitizes and optimizes workflows in hospitals with cutting-edge technology in a user-friendly way. With our software, we enable hospitals, doctors & nurses to provide their patients with the best possible care.
We are growing fast and want to keep expanding our team and business to fundamentally digitize healthcare for the better. We appreciate different backgrounds and see diversity as one of our strengths.




THE TEAM AND ROLE

As a Working Student Compliance Management (all genders), you provide hands-on operational support to the Compliance Manager in the implementation and ongoing monitoring of certification requirements in accordance with DIN EN ISO (ISO 27001/ ISO 9001 / ISO 13485 and BSI C5) standards. The role contributes to ensuring that compliance processes are effectively embedded and maintained across the organization. In addition, the working student supports the preparation and provision of regulatory information (e.g. GDPR), guidance, and documentation for product development teams. This position helps bridge regulatory requirements and day-to-day product development activities to support compliant and high-quality solutions.




YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Support the Compliance Manager in the implementation, documentation, and monitoring of certification and regulatory requirements (e.g. DIN EN ISO, BSI C5)

 - Assist in maintaining compliance-related processes, policies, and evidence in collaboration with engineering, product management, IT, and project teams

 - Prepare, structure, and update regulatory documentation and internal training materials for product developers and other stakeholders

 - Support audits, internal reviews, and continuous improvement activities by gathering information, tracking action items, and ensuring timely follow-up across teams




YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

 - Currently studying in industrial engineering, a technical field of study, or law

 - Initial experience or strong interest in structured, process-oriented work (e.g. through internships, student jobs, or university projects)

 - Confident use of common office tools (e.g. Microsoft Office, Notion), and the ability to document processes and requirements clearly and systematically

 - Strong communication and collaboration skills, paired with a high level of accuracy, reliability, and a pragmatic, hands-on, self-managed working style suitable for a startup environment

 - Very good communication skills in German and English




YOUR BENEFITS

 - Variety in your tasks and the opportunity to take ownership

 - Flexible working hours

 - Support for your personal development through continuous learning and feedback and high chances to transition into a full-time role if the collaboration is successful

 - An international, informal team structure in a motivating start-up atmosphere, allowing you to participate directly in our customer growth story and the overall success of Avelios

 - Ability to gain valuable hands-on experience in a startup backed by leading international VCs

 - A monthly meal allowance up to 75 euros, and Corporate Benefits that give you access to discounts from a wide range of providers in various areas

source_jobs.title: Clinical Change Manager (all genders) - Nursing or Medical Background
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT US

Our mission at Avelios is to unlock clinical data to power seamless healthcare operations for better patient care. To do so, we have built a modular software platform that digitizes and optimizes workflows in hospitals with cutting-edge technology in a user-friendly way. With our software, we enable hospitals, doctors & nurses to provide their patients with the best possible care.
We are growing fast and want to keep expanding our team and business to fundamentally digitize healthcare for the better. We appreciate different backgrounds and see diversity as one of our strengths.




THE TEAM AND ROLE

As a Clinical Change Manager (all genders), you will play a key role in supporting hospitals during the introduction of our Avelios software solution.
This role is especially suited for nurses, physicians, or other clinically trained professionals who know hospital workflows from hands-on experience and want to drive digital transformation in healthcare. You will work closely with nursing teams, physicians, and hospital stakeholders on-site, helping them adopt new digital workflows during trainings, go-lives, and hypercare phases.
This is a highly operational and people-centered position with frequent on-site presence in hospitals. You will act as a central interface between clinical end users — especially nurses and physicians — and our internal implementation, product, and engineering teams. Your success will be measured by smooth transitions, high user acceptance, and stable operations after go-live.
This role includes frequent travel to customer sites.




YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Support hospitals during the rollout of Avelios, with a strong focus on clinical adoption by nursing and medical teams

 - Coordinate end-user trainings and hypercare phases together with internal teams and external partners

 - Conduct on-site and remote trainings for clinical users before go-live, tailored to the workflows of nurses, physicians, and interdisciplinary teams

 - Act as the main point of contact for clinical users during go-live and stabilization phases in hospitals

 - Translate the needs, questions, and concerns of nurses, physicians, and other hospital stakeholders into actionable insights for our internal teams

 - Manage change in the clinical environment by building trust, fostering acceptance, and addressing concerns proactively

 - Collect, analyze, and document user feedback, issues, and improvement opportunities from real hospital workflows

 - Communicate and prioritize insights for internal teams to continuously improve our software and rollout processes




YOUR QUALIFICATIONS

 - Several years of hands-on experience in a hospital setting, ideally as a nurse, physician, or other clinically trained professional, with a strong understanding of clinical workflows across wards, outpatient departments, emergency care, operating rooms, or similar environments

 - Solid knowledge of nursing and medical documentation, medication workflows, and interdisciplinary collaboration between nursing staff, physicians, and other clinical teams

 - Strong empathy for the needs, challenges, and communication styles of clinical users, combined with excellent communication skills and confidence in working with diverse hospital stakeholders

 - Practical experience with clinical IT systems, digital documentation, implementation projects, or user support is a strong plus

 - Structured, solution-oriented working style with experience in documentation or ticketing systems, high ownership, resilience, and a hands-on mentality

 - Fluent German and good working proficiency in English
   
   


YOUR BENEFITS

 - Variety in your tasks and the opportunity to take ownership

 - An international, informal team structure in a motivating start-up atmosphere, allowing you to participate directly in our customer growth story and the overall success of Avelios

 - Ability to gain valuable hands-on experience in a startup backed by leading internat

notes: High volume of tech roles, including multiple software engineering positions. Strong focus on healthcare and clinical data.
